_comment: ILLUSTRATIVE STARTING POINT (docs/api/deployment/README.md) — a partial Keycloak realm export. Import via the admin console (Realm settings > Action > Partial import) or `kcadm.sh create realms -f this-file.json`, then fill in real users/LDAP federation. Role names below are exactly what inspecto-security's RoleMapper (rbac-groundwork.md §3) understands.

realm.name: admin
realm.description: User admin, external-system onboarding (grants beyond the current 3 named capabilities — rbac-groundwork.md §4 open Q1, not yet enforced server-side).

clients._comment: Public SPA client — Authorization Code + PKCE, no client secret (a browser app cannot keep one).
clients.name: Inspecto SPA

protocolMappers._comment: Flattens realm roles into a top-level 'roles' claim (RoleMapper's default `-Dauth.oidc.rolesClaim`). Keycloak's own realm_access.roles nesting is also read as a fallback, so this mapper is convenience, not a hard requirement.
protocolMappers.name: realm-roles-flat

clients._comment: The OAuth2 audience the backend checks via -Dauth.oidc.audience=inspecto-api. Not a login client itself — inspecto-spa's tokens carry this audience via an audience mapper (add one on inspecto-spa in a real realm, or use this clientId as the intended audience directly, per Keycloak's client-scope/audience conventions for the deployment at hand).
clients.name: Inspecto Control Plane (resource server audience)
